channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canada Announces $100 Million in New Aid for Palestinians in Gaza and the West Bank
item.description: Canada has announced an additional $100 million in funding to support Palestinians affected by worsening humanitarian conditions in the Gaza Strip and escalating violence in the West Bank. Foreign Aff...

item.title: Interior Secretary Doug Burgum Draws Criticism for Calling Response to Nuclear Threats 'Exciting Times'
item.description: A political controversy erupted after U.S. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum described current geopolitical developments involving Iran and potential nuclear threats as leading to 'exciting times ahead' ...

item.title: Alberta sport shooters warn federal handgun restrictions could threaten the future of IPSC competitions
item.description: Competitive handgun shooters in Alberta say federal handgun restrictions introduced in 2023 are placing the long-term future of their sport at risk. Members of the International Practical Shooting Con...

item.title: Quezon City Marks Third Rainbow Graduation and Highlights Inclusive Adoption Opportunities for LGBTQIA+ Community
item.description: The Quezon City government conducted its third 'rainbow' graduation ceremony on June 11, 2026 as part of its efforts to promote inclusivity and gender-affirming support for members of the LGBTQIA+ com...

item.title: Former DHS Official Miles Taylor Reflects on Trump Anecdote as Illustration of Presidential Power
item.description: A Raw Story article reports on comments made by Miles Taylor, a former chief of staff at the U.S. Department of Homeland Security during Donald Trump's first presidential term. Writing for The i Paper...

item.title: SpaceX shares surge after record IPO, lifting Elon Musk’s net worth above US$1 trillion
item.description: SpaceX made a highly anticipated debut on the Nasdaq, with its shares opening 11 per cent above the initial public offering price of US$135 per share. The strong market response valued the company at ...

item.title: Father of Texas murder victim makes racist remarks after gag order is lifted in high-profile case
item.description: Jeff Metcalf, the father of Texas teenager Austin Metcalf, made a series of racist and inflammatory remarks during a podcast interview shortly after a judge lifted a gag order in the murder case invol...

item.title: SpaceX makes record-breaking Wall Street debut amid expectations of massive valuation surge
item.description: SpaceX has made its long-anticipated debut on the Nasdaq, with investors signalling strong demand that could push the company’s valuation well beyond $US2 trillion, potentially reaching about $US2.8 t...

item.title: Ruto Pledges School Safety Reforms and Support for Families After Utumishi Girls Academy Fire Tragedy
item.description: President William Ruto has pledged decisive government action following the tragic fire at Utumishi Girls Academy that claimed the lives of 16 students. Speaking through a condolence message read by E...

item.title: Washington National Opera Files $17 Million Lawsuit Against Kennedy Center Over Disputed Funds
item.description: The Washington National Opera (WNO) has filed a lawsuit against the Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts, seeking approximately $17 million that it claims was collected and held for the opera's bene...

item.title: US face Paraguay in World Cup opener as Canada prepare for home tournament pressure
item.description: The United States and Canada are both gearing up for their opening matches at the FIFA World Cup 2026, with coaches stressing readiness, mentality, and the challenges ahead. US head coach Mauricio Poc...

item.title: Traditional Ottoman Wooden Ferris Wheels Preserved in Early 20th-Century Photographs
item.description: The article explores a collection of historical photographs showing traditional wooden Ferris wheels that were common across the Ottoman Empire during the early twentieth century. Known in Turkish as ...

item.title: A personal travel reflection on discovering and falling in love with London
item.description: This travel narrative follows the personal journey of a South African writer reflecting on her deep emotional connection with London, formed over years of fascination and repeated visits. The writer r...

item.title: Federal Judge Refuses to Vacate Ruling Criticizing Jeanine Pirro's Investigation of Jerome Powell
item.description: A federal judge has rejected an effort by U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro to erase a previous court ruling that sharply criticized her criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. Pirr...

item.title: ACT Liberals propose 50-cent public transport fares, meth recriminalisation and stricter budget controls
item.description: ACT Opposition Leader Mark Parton used his budget reply speech to outline several policy commitments that the ACT Liberals would pursue if elected in 2028. Key proposals included introducing 50-cent f...

item.title: Trump’s Late DNI Nominee Change Frustrates Senate Republicans and Jeopardizes Surveillance Bill
item.description: President Donald Trump’s decision to withdraw his original nominee for Director of National Intelligence (DNI) and replace him with a new candidate has created significant frustration among Senate Rep...

item.title: Bafana Bafana’s tactical setup questioned after 2-0 World Cup loss to Mexico
item.description: Bafana Bafana’s opening match at the 2026 Fifa World Cup ended in a disappointing 2-0 defeat to Mexico at the Estadio Azteca in Mexico City, leaving head coach Hugo Broos with plenty to reflect on. Th...

item.title: Study Links North Atlantic Cold Region to Continued Weakening of the AMOC and Potential Climate Tipping Point
item.description: A new scientific study has provided strong evidence that the unusual cold region in the North Atlantic, located south of Greenland and often called the 'cold blob' or 'North Atlantic warming hole,' is...

item.title: National Memorial Held in Gilgil for 16 Utumishi Girls Students Killed in School Fire
item.description: A memorial service for 16 students from Utumishi Girls who lost their lives in a tragic school fire last month is being held at Gilgil Stadium in Kenya. The ceremony has drawn hundreds of mourners, in...

item.title: AFP Defends Teodoro and Rejects China's Sanctions as Political Intimidation
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) expressed strong support for Defense Secretary Gilbert 'Gibo' Teodoro Jr. after China imposed sanctions on him and members of his family. China announced that...
